mnemonics: Grow, Split, Merge: Like cultivating a garden - first plant seeds (grow), then divide overgrown patches (split), and finally combine similar plots (merge).
keyPoints: Region Growing starts with seed points and aggregates neighboring pixels with similar properties.
Region Splitting (top-down) starts with the whole image and recursively divides it into quadrants if they are not homogeneous.
Region Merging (bottom-up) combines adjacent regions that are similar according to a criterion, often used after splitting.
commonMistakes: Choosing poor initial seed points for region growing, which can lead to under-segmentation or over-segmentation.
Setting similarity thresholds too strictly (causing over-segmentation) or too loosely (causing under-segmentation) for merging.
Forgetting that region splitting alone often results in an oversegmented result that requires a subsequent merging step.
